query_normalized: investigating the impact of network centrality on the consolidation of motor memories neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by providing same-mechanism evidence for sleep-dependent memory consolidation mediated by brain network dynamics (EEG connectivity/spindles), and criterion (b) by measuring the dependent variable of memory consolidation within the neuroscience domain, serving as foundational prior work for the user's specific investigation into motor memory and centrality.
